stage: A group of students is huddled outside Mr. Haney's office.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Mr. Haney is about to post a list that tells us which teacher we'll get this year.

stage: Mr. Haney enters with the list. The students gasp and murmur in anticipation.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Uh-oh.

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: Oh! Forgot a tack. [reenters office]

line.speaker: Francine and Binky
line.dialogue: Huh?!

stage: Students groan.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: There are three teachers for third grade: Mrs Sweetwater, she's nice; Mrs. Fink, she's fun...

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Billy, name three oceans.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: ...and Mr. Ratburn, the toughest, scariest teacher in school—no, in the whole world—no, in the whole history of the world.

stage: Mr. Haney pins the list to the billboard.

line.speaker: Student
line.dialogue: Oh, here comes the list.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Let's go look. Arthur, we're... in the Rat's class!

stage: Arthur, Buster, Binky, Brain, and Maria scream. Binky faints.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (V.O.) Arthur and the Real Mr. Ratburn

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Maybe you read the list wrong, Francine. Let me look. You're right. It's Ratburn!

stage: On the baseball field

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: chanting fearfully Ratburn... Ratburn... Ratburn... Ratburn... Ratburn... Ratburn...

stage: On the soccer field

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: chanting in a confusing way Ratburn... Ratburn... Ratburn... Ratburn...

stage: A soccer ball bounces off her head and lands in the goal.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: continues Ratburn... Ratburn...

stage: Walking down the sidewalk

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: chants in a sad way while walking with the others Ratburn... Ratburn...

line.speaker: Mrs. Powers
line.dialogue: Free samples of a new flavor, Rocky Rope. All you can eat!

line.speaker: Brain
line.dialogue: Maybe he's not as rigorous as everyone says.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Prunella had him last year. Let's ask her.

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: [laughs] "Is the Rat bad?" Does a bull fight? He eats nails for breakfast...

stage: The others gasp. The imagination ending music plays by mistake to make the imagination start.

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: ...without milk!

stage: In an imagine spot, Ratburn is seen pouring a bowl of nails. He eats a spoonful, stopping to pull a screw out of his mouth and toss it away. He then eats the spoon. The imagination beginning music plays by mistake to make the imagination end.

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: And make sure you never answer any questions wrong... or he'll send you to death row.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Death row?

stage: Arthur is seen locked in a dungeon.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Have you done your math exercises?

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: I did the first 5000, sir.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Then you have 9,995,000 left! Get to work! And after that, you have 37,000,000 spelling words to memorize. And you spiders, get moving!

stage: Spiders are seen spinning a web that looks like a map of the world.

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: And he's also a weird vampire... with hypnotic, magic powers.

stage: The mistakable imagination ending music plays again. The organ music plays. Ratburn is seen opening his casket at the front of the class.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: You are in my power.

line.speaker: Students
line.dialogue: We do not like recess.

stage: Back to reality by the same mistakable imagination beginning tune.

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: Well, good luck!

stage: all groan
In Ratburn's classroom
Arthur gasps before shushing happens by a student in the distance.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Good morning, everyone. Welcome to grade three. My name is Mr. Ratburn.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: He doesn't look so bad.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: It's probably a trick.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Let's start with a two-minute math quiz to see what you remember from second grade.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I told you it was a trick.

stage: Ratburn hands out the quiz. Arthur sees Mrs. Sweetwater's class having fun and eating popcorn, while Mrs. Fink's is exercising to the beat of a boombox.

line.speaker: Mrs. Fink
line.dialogue: Welcome to the first day of school. School is cool! School is cool!

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Arthur Read, you'd better concentrate on those problems before time's up.

stage: The scene changes to the end of the day, at 3:00 PM. The other classes burst out the doors and run outside at the final bell, while Mr. Ratburn calmly opens his door.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Class, you may now file out in an orderly fashion. Quietly. No talking. Arthur Read, are you standing in your proper alphabetical position?

stage: Arthur moves behind Francine. Cut to the baseball field.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Boy, Ratburn's tough! He gave us homework on the first day.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Get with it, Arthur. We're not dealing with a human being here. This is serious!

stage: Binky hits the ball into the bushes under Ratburn's window

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: You find it yet?

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Hello, this is Mr. Ratburn.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Shhh!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Uh-oh.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Yes, I have all the eyes I need, but I'm completely out of heads. I need heads. I can wait for girls' heads, but I must have boys' heads immediately.

stage: Arthur starts to leave

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Wait, he'll see us! And we have... boys' heads!

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: There's no time! I need them now. I'll have to get them myself. [hangs up]

stage: Arthur and Buster scream and take off in a puff of dust, running really fast to the Sugar Bowl.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: What are you doing?

stage: Arthur and Buster scream.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Ratburn. He—He's looking for body parts—heads! Boys' heads!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: You must have heard wrong. A vampire doesn't need heads.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: This is no ordinary vampire.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: He's beyond vampire.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: What's beyond a vampire?

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: A Ratburn!

stage: Ratburn is seen across the street walking to a Hardware Store. Arthur and Buster scream and hide behind Francine.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Hey! What—

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Stand still. He wants boy heads, not girls'.

stage: The three look in the window of the hardware store and see Ratburn picking up a box of nails.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Breakfast!

stage: Ratburn picks up a saw and puts it in his bag. Arthur and Buster hold their necks.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: What do you think he's gonna do with that saw?

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: I read a book where kids discovered a scary mystery like this and became detectives to solve it.

stage: Ratburn exits the store. The three scream and run into an alley in a cartoonish manner.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: We should probably investigate.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: You know, we have a lot of homework to do.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: You're just a fraidy-cat.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Arthur, we have to choose: face horrifying, scary danger or do our homework.

line.speaker: Francine and Arthur
line.dialogue: Horrifying, scary danger.

stage: The three surreptitiously observe Ratburn checking out books at the library, reading a newspaper, and refereeing a basketball game.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I'm bored. He's not doing anything weird. You probably heard him talking about heads of lettuce.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Boy heads of lettuce? I don't think so, Francine.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Oh, yeah? It's probably the term grocers use for the rotten ones. I'm gonna do my homework.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Arthur, look!

stage: Ratburn is seen wheeling a large suitcase down a flight of stairs. Suddenly, it opens and an arm pokes out. Ratburn puts it back and closes the case, when suddenly his watch beeps. It's 3:45. He rushes off with the suitcase.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: [afraid] M-Maybe we should go do our homework.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Come on.

stage: At the entrance to the carnival

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: He went into the carnival.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Of course he went into the carnival. Look at all those boy heads!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: We've got to warn them.

stage: They go into a mirror funhouse, and Mr. Ratburn approaches.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Oh, no! He's got us! We're trapped!

stage: Mr. Ratburn walks past without noticing the boys...

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Look! There he goes! Come on! He went into that booth.

stage: Arthur and Buster enter the booth and get sprayed with water guns. They leave.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Okay, it was that one.

stage: Inside that booth, Mrs. MacGrady is taking a picture of Mr. Haney sitting on a horse.

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: This is very authentic, Mrs. MacGrady, but I'd prefer a real action pose.

stage: Arthur bumps a cactus cutout, which falls and startles the horse. The camera captures him right before he falls off, resulting in an unintentional action pose.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Sorry, sir.

stage: Back outside

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Okay, I pick this time.

stage: He points to another booth, inside which can be seen two silhouettes; one of them is Mr. Ratburn. Arthur and Buster gasp.

line.speaker: Arthur and Buster
line.dialogue: One... two... three... go!

stage: They run inside, finding themselves on stage in front of an audience of kids, including the rest of Mr. Ratburn's class.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Run away!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: You're not safe! He'll get you!

stage: The audience laughs. The boys look and see two life-size marionettes of Jack of "Jack and the Beanstalk" and a radio.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: [as Jack] Hey, you're not in this story.

line.speaker: Puppet radio
line.dialogue: And you're doing my lines! I warn Jack to run away, but not till he hears "Fee-fi-fo—" you know the drill.

stage: The audience laughs again.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Arthur, Buster, get off the stage! [in Jack's voice] Okay, fellas, when you leave that castle, make a left, down that beanstalk, and you'll be back in Elwood City.

stage: Backstage

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Wow! The Rat does kids' puppet shows?

line.speaker: Puppet radio
line.dialogue: Jack, you're disorganized. You'd forget your head if it wasn't attached.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: [as Jack] Uh-uh! Would not!

stage: Jack's head falls off.

line.speaker: Puppet radio
line.dialogue: Grab your head and hide! Here comes the giant!

stage: As the audience screams, Jack's cracked head rolls over to Arthur and Buster.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Arthur, look!

stage: The headless Jack puppet wanders off stage as the giant enters.

line.speaker: Giant
line.dialogue: Fee-fi-fo-fum!

line.speaker: Audience
line.dialogue: Ew! Gross!

stage: Backstage

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: [inspecting his damaged puppet head] They always crack when I do that. I must go through 50 boy heads a year.

line.speaker: Arthur and Buster
line.dialogue: Boy heads!

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: I can never have enough boy heads.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: We didn't know you made puppets.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: [reassembling a head for Jack] Can't talk, boys—I have 40 seconds to get Jack ready. Must always stay on schedule because of all I do.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: You do a lot! You're a teacher, a coach, you work on the school paper... [laughs] I can't even find time to do my homework and eat!

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: You need to learn to organize your time. I've heard that some people think I give a lot of homework.

line.speaker: Arthur and Buster
line.dialogue: We never heard that.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: But once you've learned good work habits, you'll discover—

line.speaker: Puppet radio
line.dialogue: Jack! Where are you?

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: [as Jack] Just a second! The cat rolled my head into the litter box. [to Arthur and Buster] You're capable of more than you think. I'll teach you how to do that homework and then have more time for things you enjoy.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Like how you do all that stuff plus puppets?

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Exactly. [checks watch] Ah! Two seconds early. Self-discipline is as important to learn as two plus two. [as Jack] Okay, I got my head on straight! [goes back on stage with Jack's head backwards]

line.speaker: Puppet radio
line.dialogue: Hey, man, your head's on straight, but your body is backwards!

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: Please don't stand there. I have to concentrate on my work.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Oh. Sorry. [to Buster] Boy, were we wrong.

line.speaker: Ratburn
line.dialogue: By the way, boys, I can't wait to see your Geography projects tomorrow.

stage: Arthur and Buster scream as the "Here we go again" music plays, and then it irises out.
